title: Dryopteris adenorachis
taxonomy: Dryopteris adenorachis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Dryopteris adenorachis includes being in the genus Dryopteris, which comprises approximately 250 species of ferns. Dryopteris adenorachis is further classified into the order Polypodiales, which is a large group of ferns that have two distinct types of leaves. As a member of this order, Dryopteris adenorachis exhibits characteristics common to other ferns within the Polypodiales order.
uses: Dryopteris adenorachis is a species of fern used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Dryopteris adenorachis, also known as the Korean fern or autumn fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus, characterized by its delicate fronds and ability to thrive in a variety of environments.
